domain: Wire planningPilePreset / reviewPilePreset / executionCoordination(or Coordinator)PilePreset behind real @dogpile/sdk run() calls. Protostar stays authority; piles supply bounded opinions. Pile output flows through existing admission paths; failure modes match fixture refusal artifacts; capability envelope enforces budget. dogpile-adapter remains zero-fs.

sections.title: A. Invocation surface — where the SDK runs

questions.title: Who calls @dogpile/sdk run()?
questions.context: PILE-06 locks dogpile-adapter as zero-fs. Today packages/dogpile-adapter/src/index.ts only declares presets + parse helpers. The actual run() call (network I/O) needs an owner. apps/factory-cli is the only fs/I/O-permitted app; @protostar/repo is the other authority. Putting the run() call inside dogpile-adapter is fine if it stays I/O-free (network is allowed, fs is forbidden).

options.label: dogpile-adapter exposes runFactoryPile(mission, ctx)
options.description: Adapter owns SDK invocation. Network only — no fs (still passes the contract test). factory-cli passes provider, signal, budget; receives result + trace. Symmetric with Phase 4's createLmstudioCoderAdapter pattern.

options.label: factory-cli imports SDK directly; adapter stays preset-only
options.description: Adapter only declares presets + parsers. factory-cli/src/main.ts calls run({ ...preset, intent, provider }). Fewer indirection layers; preset module remains a 'config object' library, no runtime.

options.label: New @protostar/pile-runtime package
options.description: Third package owns SDK invocation, sits between adapter (presets) and factory-cli (orchestration). Useful if multiple apps later, or if pile-specific retry/cancel logic gets fat.

questions.title: SDK entrypoint shape: run() vs stream() vs Dogpile.pile()
questions.context: @dogpile/sdk exports run() (single-call Promise<RunResult>), stream() (event-based with StreamHandle), and Dogpile.pile() (high-level builder). Phase 9 'inspect' will eventually want event-level traces; Phase 8 evaluation may want intermediate signals.

options.label: run() — single Promise<RunResult>
options.description: Simplest. Returns final + trace + accounting. No live event consumption in v0.1. Cancellation via AbortSignal still works.

options.label: stream() — consume RunEvent stream
options.description: Lets Phase 5/6 emit ReviewLifecycleEvents as the pile progresses (per-turn, per-judge). More plumbing now; better operator surface later.

options.label: Dogpile.pile() builder
options.description: Higher-level; tighter coupling to SDK shape. Useful if we want SDK-managed retries/replay; risk if SDK API churns.

questions.title: Provider construction & sharing
questions.context: SDK takes a ConfiguredModelProvider. Phase 4 already wires createOpenAICompatibleProvider equivalent for LM Studio (Qwen3-Coder). Phase 5 Q-10 wires a Qwen3-80B judge via lmstudio-adapter. Piles need a provider too — same LM Studio endpoint, different model ids per agent.

options.label: Single shared LM Studio provider, per-agent model ids
options.description: factory-cli builds one createOpenAICompatibleProvider({ baseUrl }), each AgentSpec carries its own model id. Reuses preflight from Phase 4 Q-13.

options.label: Per-pile-kind provider
options.description: Planning pile uses one provider config, review pile another, exec-coord another. Necessary if different baseUrls/keys; over-config if all hit one LM Studio.

options.label: AgentSpec.provider override
options.description: Each AgentSpec optionally specifies its own provider. Most flexible; most config surface.

questions.title: Mode-selection flag granularity
questions.context: ROADMAP success criterion #1: '--planning-mode pile runs a real planning pile'. Today fixtures live in examples/planning-results/. Review pile / exec-coord pile have no precedent flag. Need to decide whether one switch covers all piles or each is independently gated.

options.label: Single --piles-mode {fixture|live} global flag
options.description: One switch flips all three piles. Simplest operator surface; matches dark-factory dichotomy (fixtures for tests, live for real runs).

options.label: Per-pile flags: --planning-mode, --review-mode, --exec-coord-mode
options.description: Independent control. Useful for partial dogfooding (e.g., live planning + fixture review). More config; matches roadmap wording (singular --planning-mode).

options.label: factory-config.json piles.{planning,review,executionCoordination}.mode
options.description: Per-pile-kind in config. CLI flag overrides. Symmetric with Phase 4 Q-09 factory-config.json layout.

questions.title: Default mode for v0.1
questions.context: Phase 10 dogfood needs ≥10 consecutive cosmetic-tweak runs with ≥80% pr-ready. Live piles introduce variance; fixtures are deterministic. Tradeoff: ship live by default proves the loop, but fixture-default keeps CI stable.

options.label: Default fixture; --live opts in
options.description: Safe CI; live mode is operator-explicit. Phase 10 dogfood toggles live for the dogfood runs.

options.label: Default live; --fixture opts in
options.description: Dark factory leans live. CI uses --fixture explicitly. Tests pin fixture mode.

options.label: Default depends on archetype
options.description: cosmetic-tweak defaults live (it's the v0.1 dogfood archetype); other (stub) archetypes default fixture.

questions.title: Fixture-mode fallback when live fails
questions.context: Roadmap: 'Pile timeout / budget exhaustion fails the pile (not the run); fixture-mode fallback still works'. Ambiguous: does live-mode auto-fall-back to fixture on failure, or 'fixture-mode still works' just means fixture-mode runs untouched?

options.label: No auto-fallback; live failure = no-admission artifact (fail the pile)
options.description: Strict. Live failures hit refusal-artifact path. Fixture mode is a separate code path operators choose; no implicit fallback. Matches admission-discipline posture.

options.label: Auto-fallback to fixture on transient pile failure
options.description: Resilience. Pile timeout → use the latest matching fixture. Hides live failures; muddies the dark-factory contract.

options.label: Operator opt-in fallback via --pile-fallback fixture
options.description: Explicit fallback gated by flag. Off by default; on for dogfood iteration when LM Studio is flaky.

questions.title: Pile output directory layout
questions.context: PILE-06 forbids fs in adapter; factory-cli writes. Phase 5 Q-17 uses runs/{id}/review/iter-{N}/. Need a parallel layout for piles. Live piles produce: pile-result.json (parsed), trace.json (replay), refusal.json (on failure). Multiple iterations possible per run (planning once, review once-per-loop-iteration, exec-coord on demand).

options.label: runs/{id}/piles/{kind}/iter-{N}/{result,trace,refusal}.json
options.description: Per-pile-kind subdir; per-iteration subdir mirrors Phase 5 review layout. {kind} ∈ planning|review|execution-coordination. iter-N corresponds to loop attempt.

options.label: Co-locate under stage dir: runs/{id}/planning/pile-result.json, runs/{id}/review/iter-{N}/pile-result.json
options.description: Pile result lives next to its consuming admission artifact (planning beside planning-admission, review beside iter-N review).

options.label: Flat runs/{id}/piles.jsonl
options.description: Append-only line per pile invocation. Easy to grep; harder to fetch full trace blobs.

questions.title: Trace persistence policy
questions.context: @dogpile/sdk run() returns a Trace (full event/decision log). Useful for Phase 9 inspect and Phase 8 evaluation. Costly in disk: a planning pile may produce 100s of KB to MB.

options.label: Always persist full trace.json
options.description: Maximum forensic surface. Phase 9 inspect can replay. Disk cost real.

options.label: Persist only on failure / non-pass
options.description: Trace is debug evidence; success runs only persist parsed result + accounting summary. Reduces disk; loses positive-case forensics.

options.label: Configurable: factory-config.json piles.persistTrace: 'always'|'on-failure'|'never'
options.description: Default on-failure; operators can set 'always' for dogfood weeks.

questions.title: Adapter zero-fs contract test
questions.context: PILE-06 success criterion: 'dogpile-adapter still does zero filesystem I/O (contract test)'. Phase 2 Plan 10 already includes 'authority-no-fs regression'. Need a concrete enforcement strategy.

options.label: Static import-graph test: no fs/path/url imports in dogpile-adapter source
options.description: Grep-based test in @protostar/dogpile-adapter that scans src/**/*.ts for import patterns. Symmetric with admission-e2e static checks.

options.label: Runtime test: stub fs.* and assert no calls during pile invocation
options.description: Wraps node:fs with a throwing proxy in a test, invokes a pile. Catches transitive fs use through deps.

options.label: Both — static (cheap, in-package) + runtime (admission-e2e)
options.description: Defense-in-depth. Static catches obvious imports; runtime catches transitive. Mirrors Phase 2/3 layered enforcement.

questions.title: Capability envelope vs preset budget reconciliation
questions.context: Each FactoryPilePreset already declares budget: { maxTokens, timeoutMs }. Capability envelope (signed in ConfirmedIntent, schema 1.3.0+) carries network/budget caps. PILE-05 mandates 'pile invocations carry the capability envelope's budget'. Need a precedence rule.

options.label: Envelope clamps preset (intersect: min of each)
options.description: Preset proposes; envelope caps. If preset says 24000 tokens but envelope budget says 12000, pile runs with 12000. Symmetric with Phase 2 precedence kernel.

options.label: Envelope replaces preset (envelope is the only source)
options.description: Preset budget is ignored at runtime; only envelope counts. Cleanest authority story; preset becomes pure metadata.

options.label: New envelope field budget.piles.{kind}: { maxTokens, timeoutMs, maxCalls }
options.description: Explicit per-pile-kind envelope budget. Schema bump 1.3.0 → 1.4.0 (or coordinated with Phase 5 Q-12 maxRepairLoops bump). Most operator control; biggest schema surface.

questions.title: Cancellation / AbortSignal threading
questions.context: factory-cli already wires SIGINT/sentinel cancel through Phase 4 (Q-16). Long-running piles must respond to the same signal. SDK run() accepts an AbortSignal in EngineOptions.

options.label: Single AbortController per run; passed to every pile + adapter call
options.description: One SIGINT cancels everything. Simplest; matches existing factory-cli pattern.

options.label: Hierarchical: per-pile AbortController parented to run-level
options.description: Allows pile-level timeout to cancel only that pile (not the whole run); SIGINT still cascades. Maps to Q-10c per-kind budgets.

questions.title: Pile failure refusal artifact location & shape
questions.context: Roadmap: 'Schema parse errors on pile output produce the same no-admission artifacts as fixture parse errors'. Today fixture parse failures emit refusals to .protostar/refusals.jsonl + per-run refusal JSON (Phase 1 Q-08 layout). PILE-04 broadens to include timeout, schema parse, all-candidates-rejected.

options.label: Reuse existing planning/review refusal-artifact format; new sourceOfTruth: 'PlanningPileResult' (already exists) + 'ReviewPileResult' + 'ExecutionCoordinationPileResult'
options.description: Refusal artifacts are stage-uniform. Pile-specific failure reasons appear in the existing reason field. .protostar/refusals.jsonl gains pile entries.

options.label: New piles-specific refusal layout: runs/{id}/piles/{kind}/refusal.json + .protostar/pile-refusals.jsonl
options.description: Separate refusal stream for piles. Cleaner per-stage analytics; duplicates the index pattern.

questions.title: Pile failure taxonomy
questions.context: Failure classes: (i) timeout (preset/envelope timeoutMs hit); (ii) budget exhausted (token/call cap); (iii) schema parse error (output not valid pile result); (iv) all candidates rejected (judge/convergence terminate without acceptable output); (v) network/transport error (LM Studio unreachable, retries exhausted); (vi) cancellation (SIGINT). Need to decide how granular the discriminator is.

options.label: Six-variant union: pile-timeout | pile-budget | pile-parse | pile-rejected | pile-network | pile-cancelled
options.description: Maximum discriminator surface. Phase 8 evaluation can analyze cause distribution. Most code branches; clearest evidence.

options.label: Three buckets: pile-budget-exhausted (i+ii) | pile-output-invalid (iii+iv) | pile-transport (v+vi)
options.description: Coarser. Easier to map to existing refusal taxonomy; loses some forensic detail.

options.label: Mirror SDK's NormalizedStopReason exactly
options.description: Use SDK's own stop-reason union as the wire format. Tightest SDK coupling; lowest mapping cost; reshapes if SDK churns.

sections.title: F. Composition with Phase 5 (review pile + execution-coordination pile)

questions.title: Review pile vs Phase 5 Q-10 single-Qwen judge — replace, augment, or coexist?
questions.context: Phase 5 Q-10 LOCK: 'Minimal panel-of-one stub: a single local LM Studio judge (Qwen3-80B) wired now via lmstudio-adapter (or extension to lmstudio-adapter). Phase 5 ships a real one-judge implementation; Phase 8 expands to N=2 panel + consensus math without changing the loop seam.' PILE-02 NOW says 'reviewPilePreset is invoked after mechanical review and composes with mechanical verdict'. Phase 8 also expands to a panel. There are now THREE candidate model-review code paths. Need to reconcile before planning.

options.label: Phase 6 review pile REPLACES Phase 5 Q-10 single-Qwen judge
options.description: ModelReviewer interface (Phase 5 Q-10) is implemented by reviewPilePreset invocation in Phase 6. Phase 5 ships an adapter passthrough or a fixture-only implementation; Phase 6 wires the real review pile. Phase 8 swaps in heterogeneous-local panel with consensus math. Cleanest single review path.

options.label: Coexist: Phase 5 single-Qwen runs INSIDE the review pile as one of three agents
options.description: Phase 5's Qwen3-80B judge becomes one of reviewPilePreset.agents (correctness-reviewer). Pile orchestrates broadcast + termination. Phase 8 adds the second model family + consensus.

options.label: Phase 5 single judge stays as the LOOP gate; review pile produces ADVISORY critiques attached to evidence
options.description: ReviewVerdict is computed by Phase 5's single-Qwen judge (loop-gate authority); review pile output is non-gating critique evidence (informs repair plan, doesn't change verdict). Phase 8 promotes pile to gate.

questions.title: Execution-coordination pile trigger point
questions.context: PILE-03: 'invoked when execution proposes work-slicing or repair-plan generation'. Phase 5 Q-05 created @protostar/repair as a pure deterministic transform that synthesizes a RepairPlan from review findings. 'Work-slicing' is also unspecified — is it pre-execution task decomposition or per-task subdivision?

options.label: Repair-plan generation only — pile critiques the deterministic RepairPlan from @protostar/repair
options.description: @protostar/repair runs first (deterministic fan-in); exec-coord pile reviews/refines. Pile output is admitted only if it improves repair targeting; falls back to deterministic plan otherwise.

options.label: Repair-plan generation only — pile REPLACES @protostar/repair when --piles-mode live
options.description: Live mode: exec-coord pile generates the RepairPlan. Fixture mode: @protostar/repair runs deterministically. Two implementations of the same interface.

options.label: Both: pile invoked for initial work-slicing (admitted-plan task subdivision) AND repair-plan generation
options.description: Two trigger points. Initial: pile may propose splitting a coarse admitted-plan task into finer subtasks (with admission re-check). Repair: pile authors RepairPlan. More plumbing; matches PILE-03 wording most literally.

options.label: v0.1 stub — pile is wired but always returns no-op for cosmetic-tweak archetype
options.description: Cosmetic-tweak is single-task-single-file; no slicing/repair-coordination value. Wire the seam, return empty. Phase 10 dogfood validates the seam doesn't fire harmfully.

questions.title: Preset name discrepancy: executionCoordinatorPilePreset vs executionCoordinationPilePreset
questions.context: Code today: packages/dogpile-adapter/src/index.ts exports executionCoordinatorPilePreset. ROADMAP and REQUIREMENTS PILE-03 say executionCoordinationPilePreset. Lock the canonical name now.

options.label: Rename code → executionCoordinationPilePreset (match roadmap)
options.description: Update src; no consumer churn yet (no live caller). Ship rename with Phase 6 wiring.

options.label: Rename roadmap/requirements → executionCoordinatorPilePreset (match code)
options.description: Code is shipped; doc edits cheaper. Slightly less-precise English but consistent with FactoryPileKind 'execution-coordination' kebab-case.

options.label: Keep both — alias
options.description: Avoid; introduces drift surface.

questions.title: Review-pile output assert helper
questions.context: @protostar/planning already exports assertPlanningPileResult, parsePlanningPileResult, assertCandidatePlanFromPlanningPileResult. Need parallels for review pile output, since PILE-02 says output flows through review admission.

options.label: Mirror in @protostar/review: assertReviewPileResult + parseReviewPileResult
options.description: Each domain owns its pile-output assertion. dogpile-adapter re-exports for ergonomic import. Symmetric with planning side.

options.label: Generic in dogpile-adapter: assertPileResult<T extends PileKind>
options.description: Single generic helper. Less domain-localized; risks coupling adapter to all stage types.

questions.title: ExecutionCoordinationPileResult shape
questions.context: Per Q-15, the exec-coord pile's job is repair-plan critique/generation (and possibly work-slicing). Need an output schema. Existing PlanningPileResult has { output, source } where output is a JSON string parsed into CandidatePlanGraph. Should exec-coord follow the same envelope?

options.label: Same envelope { output, source }; output is JSON-stringified RepairPlan (or RepairPlanProposal)
options.description: Symmetric with PlanningPileResult. Re-uses existing envelope/source discriminator. Repair-plan structural validation lives in @protostar/repair.

options.label: Typed envelope { repairPlan: RepairPlanProposal, source }
options.description: No JSON-string indirection. Cleaner types; loses the wire-format symmetry that the parsing test pattern relies on.
